commit.message:
Fix audio FX whiteout: bloom pump kick-only, BG flash resets each frame

- Bloom pump was driven by continuous bass energy with Math.max, causing
  runaway accumulation. Now only spikes on kicks with reduced strength.
- BG flash never reset to palette color, ratcheting toward white. Now
  resets to palette.bg every frame before lerping.
- BG flash strength reduced from 0.12 to 0.04.
- All envelopes properly zeroed when audio stops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimise survey storage: replace sparse columns with JSONB answers

Before: 12 separate columns (mostly NULL per user — student has no
subject_taught, teacher has no career_goal, etc.)

After: 2 columns only — `profession` (top-level, indexed, for filtering)
and `answers` (JSONB, GIN-indexed, holds all conditional fields).

- Add GIN index on answers for fast JSONB queries
- Update saveSurveyResponse() signature: (profession, answers)
- Update getSurveyResponses() return type to SurveyRow
- Update survey page and admin page to use new shape

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CI workflow: lint and build check on every push

Doccraft is deployed to Vercel (API routes require a server runtime
and cannot be statically exported to GitHub Pages). This workflow
runs ESLint and next build on every push to main so TypeScript and
lint errors are caught before they reach Vercel.

OPENAI_API_KEY is set to a placeholder during CI — the build step
only performs static analysis and type-checking, not API calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Friday test workflow for Sun 15 Mar + enhanced diagnostic logging

logging: capture Chrome/ChromeDriver versions per-worker, screenshot
login success/failure with page source dump, screenshot draw entry and
tee sheet visibility.

Bulletproof DB migration: raw SQL at server startup

drizzle-kit push fails on Railway (no TypeScript parser in
production container). Replace with programmatic CREATE TABLE
IF NOT EXISTS statements using the postgres driver directly.
Runs as first thing in startServer() — guaranteed to work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix recording timing: convert v3 relative deltas to v2 absolute timestamps

Asciicast v3 uses relative (delta) timestamps between events, while v2
uses absolute (elapsed since start). The conversion was missing the
accumulation step, causing playback to appear instant.

Optimizer nits: reduce event noise, improve docs, add advanced example (#4013)

* Optimizer nits: reduce event noise, improve docs, add advanced example

- Remove noisy OptimizerAutoConfigured event that fired on every
  reconcile because config is rebuilt from scratch each time (the CRD
  never has embeddingService set, the controller populates it from the
  ref). This is the normal/expected code path, not worth an event.
- Improve EmbeddingServer image field documentation to clarify that
  images must come from HuggingFace TEI.
- Fix default image reference in embedding-servers README (cpu-latest,
  not latest).
- Add vmcp_optimizer_advanced.yaml example with all optimizer and
  EmbeddingServer fields explicitly configured for production tuning.

commit.message:
fix: DOCX generation — _text() always returns single TextRun, _multiText() for \n

Root cause: _text() returning arrays broke docx.js Paragraph children
in places not using _para() flatten. Now:
- _text() replaces \n with space (always safe, always single TextRun)
- _multiText() splits on \n into TextRun array with break:1
- _para() flattens nested arrays from _multiText()
- Added blob size logging for debugging

commit.message:
fix: use temporary session tokens for JWT access instead of exposing static tokens

When accessed via JWT, generate a short-lived session token for download
links instead of the permanent access_token. Session tokens expire with
the JWT (max 24h). All download/cover/zip routes resolve session tokens
via the session_tokens table. Expired sessions auto-cleanup on ~2% of requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 2A Diplomacy Core Cleanup: 17 fixes, 69 new tests

State cleanup sweep for core diplomacy systems. 5 batches:

Batch 1 - Foundation: canonical get_war_score_for() helper replacing 5
inline sign-flip implementations, defensive_alliance BASE_DISPOSITION,
self-relation guard, nation_dp storage for AI, deepcopy coalition
serialization.

Batch 2 - War-to-Peace: cleanup_war_end() helper (clears battle_records,
decisive_battles, war_scores, war_exhaustion, cancels PURSUE orders),
battle record pruning >10 turns, active_treaties cleanup on downgrade.

Batch 3 - Armistice: 5-turn expiration system with PEACE/WAR transition
based on relations, cooldowns set on entry blocking AI proposals, gold
floor in treaty clauses with dispatch event on inability to pay.

Batch 4 - Dispatch: auto-downgrade fires dispatch event + notification,
{rejection_reaction} template slot resolved, 3 coalition dispatch
templates wired, pending dialogue voided when target joins coalition.

Batch 5 - Vassal: CS membership cleared on release, vassal diplomacy
reconciled on vassalization (auto-armistice/peace), threat_level wired
in dialogue context, sweetener value floor of 5.

New serialized fields: nation_dp, armistice_turns.
5396 tests passing (69 new, 0 failures).

commit.message:
feat: add direct insert benchmark script and CI integration (#64)

## Summary
- Add `test/benchmark/bench_direct_insert.sh` that benchmarks
parameterized UNNEST direct insert (SPI bypass) vs standard DuckDB path
across batch sizes 100/1000/10000
- Add `bench-direct-insert` Makefile target
- Add CI step for PG 18 with `continue-on-error: true`

Local results show ~9-11x speedup for the direct insert path.

TODO: Add benchmarks for read performance after direct insert and
concurrent write contention.

Closes #62

## Test plan
- [x] Run locally: `PG_CONFIG=$(pwd)/pg-17/bin/pg_config make
bench-direct-insert`
- [x] Verify output table with timing for both modes across all batch
sizes
- [x] Verify speedup ratios > 1x
- [x] Verify script exits 0 regardless of results
- [ ] CI benchmark step runs on PG 18 and does not fail the build

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

MCPRemoteProxy: Surface validation errors via Events and Status Conditions (#4024)

* Improve MCPRemoteProxy error reporting with events and status conditions

Add pre-deployment validation to the MCPRemoteProxy controller that
emits Kubernetes Events and sets a ConfigurationValid status condition
when configuration errors are detected. This replaces the previous
behavior where users had to inspect pod logs to understand why their
proxy was in CrashLoopBackOff.

Validations added:
- OIDC issuer URL scheme (HTTP vs HTTPS) with insecureAllowHTTP support
- Cedar authorization policy syntax checking
- Referenced authz ConfigMap existence and key validation
- Referenced header Secret existence validation

Closes #2289

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce scope to OIDC issuer URL validation only

Remove authz policy syntax validation, ConfigMap/Secret reference
checks, and header secret validation to keep the PR focused on
foundation code (EventRecorder, ConfigurationValid condition,
validation pattern) that can be extended later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add integration tests for Kubernetes Event emission

Test that validation failures emit Warning events (visible via
kubectl describe) and that successful validations do not emit
spurious warning events.

commit.message:
Add .gitignore and remove tracked __pycache__

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix left-shift of negative signed integers in GSM codec (#4834)

* Update third_party/gsm from 1.0-pl12 to 1.0-pl23

Update the bundled GSM 06.10 codec library from patchlevel 12 (2006) to
patchlevel 23 (2022). Notable changes:
- Fix left-shift of negative signed integer in short_term.c STEP macro
- Fix stdlib.h -> string.h include for memcpy in code.c
- Add extern "C" guards in private.h for C++ compatibility
- Fix nested comment syntax in config.h
- Whitespace cleanup across all source files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix left-shift of negative signed integers in GSM codec

Replace left-shift operators with multiplication or unsigned casts in
15 places across 6 files where signed values can be negative, which is
undefined behavior per C99 §6.5.7. Discovered by fuzz-stream with
UBSan. The upstream libgsm 1.0.23 still has these unfixed.

Files changed:
- private.h: GSM_L_MULT macro (<< 1 -> * 2)
- add.c: gsm_L_mult, gsm_L_asl, gsm_asl, gsm_L_asr, gsm_asr
- lpc.c: Autocorrelation L_ACF doubling, signal rescaling,
  Reflection_coefficients normalization
- preprocess.c: downscaling, recursive filter state, offset compensation
- rpe.c: APCM quantization and inverse quantization
- short_term.c: STEP macro in LAR decoding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stripchat_sessions UNIQUE制約をNULLS NOT DISTINCTに強化（Cookie保存の42P10エラー修正）

cast_name=NULLの重複INSERT→upsert失敗（42P10）を防止。
PostgreSQL 15のNULLS NOT DISTINCTにより、NULLも一意制約に含まれるようになった。
Migration 109を本番適用済み＋upsertテスト合格確認。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent comments on unchanged context lines and non-PR files

Three layers of defense against out-of-scope comments:

1. System prompt: new "Comment Scope" section — only comment on lines
   starting with + in the diff, not context lines or unchanged files
2. get_diff_line_sets: only include + lines in commentable set (context
   lines advance the counter but aren't commentable)
3. post_review_via_gh: filter out suggestions for files not in the PR's
   changed_files list before building inline comments

commit.message:
Fix ETA rate calculation to handle resets and interpolate window boundaries

The rate calculation previously used endpoint-to-endpoint delta, which
failed entirely when a reset (100% → 0%) fell within the averaging window.

Now sums only positive inter-sample deltas over exact wall-clock time,
interpolating at window boundaries for partial segments. Also relaxes
ETA color thresholds (green at 120% vs 150%) for more intuitive urgency.

commit.message:
fix(v1.85.21): En contacto correcto en barra progreso Maratón usando ASIGNADOS del KPI

Antes: enContacto = CON_GESTORA (no existe en maraton-kpi) → 0 → gestionado 11.7% incorrecto
Ahora: enContacto = ASIGNADOS - citados - atendidos - observados = 1,623
       gestionados = 1,623 + 719 + 0 + 844 = 3,186 → 23.7% real del universo
